perturbation.original_text: Operator shall be responsible, at its sole cost and expense, for maintaining and complying with all Permits required to perform the Facility Services under this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: Operator shall only be responsible, at its sole cost and expense, for initiating the process of obtaining and maintaining the required permits to perform the Facility Services under this Agreement, while the owner will be fully responsible for ensuring the successful acquirement of permits.
perturbation.explanation: The modification introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the responsibility for obtaining permits. The original text assigns full responsibility to the operator, while the modified text splits the responsibility, assigning the initiation of the permit process to the operator and the successful acquirement of permits to the owner. This creates uncertainty and conflict, as it is unclear who bears the ultimate responsibility and risk if permits are not obtained or maintained, potentially leading to disputes and hindering the performance of Facility Services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Warranty Period, Operator shall provide services to Owner so that the Portfolio meets the Warranty Specifications and so that the BOF will not cause the Portfolio to fail to perform in accordance with the Warranty Specifications, as more fully set forth in this Article 2 (such services, collectively, the "Facility Services"). The Facilities covered under this Agreement are set forth in Appendix C hereto, which may be amended from time to time by written agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text assigns full responsibility for permits to the operator, but the changed text splits the responsibility. This directly contradicts the contradicted text because the 'Facility Services' mentioned in the contradicted text require the Operator to maintain the Portfolio and meet the Warranty Specifications, which inherently depend on the Operator having full control over permits, which is no longer the case.

perturbation.original_text: Operator shall cause Owner (and such additional parties as Owner designates in writing) to be named additional insured(s), must be written as primary policy not contributing to or in excess of any policies carried by the Owner, and each contain a waiver of subrogation endorsement, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Owner, in favor of the Owner.
perturbation.changed_text: Operator shall cause Owner (and such additional parties as Owner designates in writing) to be named additional insured(s), the cost of which shall be fully covered by the owner and must be written as primary policy not contributing to or in excess of any policies carried by the Owner, and each contain a waiver of subrogation endorsement, in form and substance reasonably satisfactory to the Owner, in favor of the Owner.
perturbation.explanation: The modification adds an in-text contradiction concerning who bears the cost of adding Owner as an additional insured. The original text implies that Operator bears this cost as part of their insurance obligations, but the changed text explicitly states that Owner will fully cover the cost. This contradicts the assumed allocation of expenses related to insurance, potentially leading to disputes over financial responsibilities and affecting the overall cost structure of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Operator shall indemnify, defend and hold harmless Owner, its members, managers, officers, directors, employees, Affiliates and agents (each, an "Owner Indemnitee") from and against any and all Indemnifiable Losses asserted against or suffered by any Owner Indemnitee arising out of any Third Party Claims against an Owner Indemnitee to the extent arising out of or in connection with (i) Operator's breach of its representations, warranties or covenants in this Agreement, (ii) the negligent or intentional acts or omissions of Operator or its subcontractors, agents or employees or others under Operator's control or (iii) a breach by Operator of its obligations hereunder; provided that Operator shall have no obligation to indemnify Owner for any negligence, fraud or willful misconduct of any Owner Indemnitee, the breach by Owner of its covenants and warranties under this Agreement or the inability to utilize any tax benefits (for the avoidance of doubt, the Grant is not considered a tax benefit).
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a direct contradiction concerning who bears the cost of insurance for the Owner. The original text implicitly assigns this cost to the Operator as part of their insurance obligations. The changed text explicitly states the Owner will cover this cost, which conflicts with the indemnity clause in the Contradicted Text, potentially affecting the Operator's indemnification obligations, as these obligations could become dependent on owner's decision to purchase insurance and at what cost. This falls under inconsistencies that lead to in-text contradiction as per the definitions.
